title: "podman ps" seems to set "--no-trunc" by default
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:

# **kind bug**

[//]: # **kind feature**

**Description**

There's an issue when we issue a ```podman ps```: the output is just unreadable. Among the issues:

- COMMAND field isn't truncated, meaning it might be far, far too long for a standard screen
- ID field shows the full ID, not the short one
- No "newline" at the end of the listing, meaning the shell prompt is on that same last line

In short: it looks like the ```--no-trunc``` is set by default, with an additional bug regarding the missing "newline" at the end of the output.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run some container

2. Run ```podman ps```

3. Enjoy the output

**Describe the results you received:**
An unreadable, far too long output without any truncated field

**Describe the results you expected:**
A human readable, truncated content output by default, and if we want to get the full output, we pass ```--no-trunc```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.10.2-dev
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**


comments.body:
Quick update: ID is truncated, and point 3 (missing newline) was corrected a couple of days ago.
Example output:
http://paste.openstack.org/show/733621/

Meaning only the "command" isn't truncated.
Does Docker by default do it differently?
@rhatdan yep, it truncates the command and other infos by default - apparently this was overlooked for the command field only, as shown in @baude patch #1728
please close if you are happy ... patch is merged now
